# terrible_dellemc_unity_tree_quota (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `state` (String) The state option is used to mention the existence of the filesystem quota tree.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `cap_unit` (String) Unit of *soft_limit* and *hard_limit* size. It defaults to `GB` if not specified.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Description of a quota tree.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `filesystem_id` (String) The ID of the filesystem for which the quota tree is created. For creation of a quota tree either *filesystem_id* or *filesystem_name* is required.
- `filesystem_name` (String) The name of the filesystem for which quota tree is created. For creation or modification of a quota tree either *filesystem_name* or *filesystem_id* is required.
- `hard_limit` (Number) Hard limitation for a quota tree on the total space available. If exceeded, users in quota tree cannot write data. Value `0` implies no limit. One of the values of *soft_limit* and *hard_limit* can be `0`, however, both cannot be both `0` during creation of a quota tree.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `nas_server_id` (String) The ID of the NAS server in which the filesystem is created. For creation of a quota tree either *filesystem_id* or *filesystem_name* is required.
- `nas_server_name` (String) The name of the NAS server in which the filesystem is created. For creation of a quota tree either *nas_server_name* or *nas_server_id* is required.
- `path` (String) The path to the quota tree. Either *tree_quota_id* or *path* to quota tree is required to create/view/modify/delete a quota tree. Path must start with a forward slash '/'.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `soft_limit` (Number) Soft limitation for a quota tree on the total space available. If exceeded, notification will be sent to users in the quota tree for the grace period mentioned, beyond which users cannot use space. Value `0` implies no limit. Both *soft_limit* and *hard_limit* cannot be `0` during creation of quota tree.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `tree_quota_id` (String) The ID of the quota tree. Either *tree_quota_id* or *path* to quota tree is required to view/modify/delete quota tree.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `get_tree_quota_details` (String) Details of the quota tree.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
